definition: A deterministic growth headwind from $900B-$1.1T in corporate debt maturing 2025-2027 with 300-500bp coupon step-ups. Its severity is a non-linear function of the rate path: manageable at consensus GDP of 2.0-2.5% but becomes a solvency wall at a growth downshift to 0-1%.
evidence: Bond maturity dates and coupon step-ups are calculable arithmetic, not debatable
Benign scenario (Fed cuts to 3.5-4.0%): drag ~10-15bp GDP
Adverse scenario (rates held at 4.5-5.0%): drag ~20-35bp, $30-50B/yr cost increase
Severe scenario (spread widening + rate holds): self-reinforcing feedback loop
pair_1 debate confirmed as highest-conviction shared claim
open_questions: Which rate path scenario will materialize?
How does the maturity wall interact with private credit refinancing channels?
Will CLO arbitrage conditions allow smooth refinancing?
